blocks.en: When several wireless networks share the same TV white space (TVWS) — the unused gaps between broadcast TV channels — they can step on each other unless something coordinates who uses which channel. IEEE 802.19.1 defines a coexistence system of cooperating software entities that talk over standard interfaces (TCP/IP, secured with SSH or TLS) to keep neighbouring networks out of each other's way. Clause 5.2 is the heart of that system: a catalogue of step-by-step procedures — request/response and announcement message exchanges — that the entities run to subscribe, register, share information, measure, negotiate, reconfigure, and shut down cleanly.
blocks.zh: 当多个无线网络共享同一片电视空白频谱（TVWS，即广播电视频道之间未被使用的空隙）时，如果没有某种机制来协调谁使用哪个信道，它们就会相互干扰。IEEE 802.19.1 定义了一个由协作软件实体组成的共存系统，这些实体通过标准接口（TCP/IP，并用 SSH 或 TLS 加密）通信，使相邻网络互不妨碍。第 5.2 条是该系统的核心：它是一份分步流程清单——由请求/响应和通告类消息交换构成——实体借此完成订阅、注册、信息共享、测量、协商、重配置以及干净地停止运行。

blocks.en: First meet the players. A WSO/GCO (white space object / geolocation-capable object, e.g. an RLSS managing a network) is the real radio network that needs to coexist. A CE (coexistence enabler) is the adapter that sits next to a WSO and translates between the WSO's world and the coexistence system. A CM (coexistence manager) makes the actual coexistence decisions for the networks it serves. A CDIS (coexistence discovery and information server) tells CMs which other networks are nearby (their 'coexistence set'). A COE (coexistence operating entity) is the gateway a CM uses to reach a CM in a different coexistence system. Almost every procedure in 5.2 is just a conversation among these five roles.
blocks.zh: 先认识一下角色。WSO/GCO（白空间对象 / 具备地理定位能力的对象，例如管理某网络的 RLSS）是需要共存的真实无线网络。CE（共存使能器）是紧邻 WSO 的适配器，在 WSO 的世界与共存系统之间做翻译。CM（共存管理器）为它所服务的网络做出真正的共存决策。CDIS（共存发现与信息服务器）告诉各 CM 附近还有哪些网络（即它们的“共存集”）。COE（共存操作实体）是某 CM 用来联系另一个共存系统中 CM 的网关。5.2 中几乎每个流程，都只是这五种角色之间的一次对话。

blocks.en:
flowchart LR
  WSO[WSO / GCO] --- CE
  CE --- CM1[CM]
  CM1 --- CDIS
  CM1 --- CM2[another CM]
  CM1 --- COE
  COE --- COE2[COE in other system]
blocks.zh:
flowchart LR
  WSO[WSO / GCO] --- CE
  CE --- CM1[CM]
  CM1 --- CDIS
  CM1 --- CM2[另一个 CM]
  CM1 --- COE
  COE --- COE2[其他系统的 COE]

blocks.en: A network's life in the coexistence system runs in a natural order. It subscribes to a service, registers its details, then continuously exchanges information until it eventually stops. (5.1) introduces the whole clause and fixes the ground rules: these procedures run among CE, CM, COE, and CDIS and with external WSO/GCO and spectrum-database entities, every exposed interface uses TCP/IP, and every interface must support both SSH and TLS. Subscription comes first (5.2.1): a CE runs the WSO subscription procedure when a network asks to start, choosing either information service (the system only advises) or management service (the system can actively reconfigure the network). The same family covers subscription updates, a CM subscribing to the CDIS's discovery service, and a CM asking a WSO to change its service type.
blocks.zh: 一个网络在共存系统中的“生命周期”遵循自然的顺序：先订阅服务，再注册自身信息，然后持续交换信息，直至最终停止。(5.1) 是整条款的导言，并确立了基本规则：这些流程在 CE、CM、COE、CDIS 之间，以及与外部的 WSO/GCO 和频谱数据库实体之间运行；每个暴露的接口都使用 TCP/IP，且每个接口都必须同时支持 SSH 和 TLS。首先是订阅 (5.2.1)：当某网络请求启动时，CE 执行 WSO 订阅流程，选择信息服务（系统只提供建议）或管理服务（系统可主动重配置该网络）。同一族流程还涵盖订阅更新、CM 向 CDIS 订阅发现服务，以及 CM 请求某 WSO 更改其服务类型。

blocks.en: Once subscribed, a network must register so the system knows it exists and where it is (5.2.2): the CE sends a registration request to its CM, the CM registers the WSO/GCO and in turn registers itself with the CDIS — and a matching deregistration procedure removes a WSO when it leaves. With registration done, a CM can build a picture of its surroundings: who its neighbours are (the coexistence set, 5.2.3), which channels a WSO is allowed to use (the available channel list, 5.2.4), and how each channel is rated — available, restricted, protected, unclassified, operating, or coexistent (channel classification, 5.2.5). Information flows two ways: a CM can pull data on request, and an entity can push an announcement when its information changes so neighbours stay current.
blocks.zh: 订阅完成后，网络必须注册，以便系统知道它的存在及位置 (5.2.2)：CE 向其 CM 发送注册请求，CM 注册该 WSO/GCO，并进而把自己注册到 CDIS——相应地还有注销流程，在 WSO 离开时将其移除。注册完成后，CM 即可建立对周围环境的认识：邻居都有谁（共存集，5.2.3）、某 WSO 被允许使用哪些信道（可用信道列表，5.2.4），以及每个信道的评级——可用、受限、受保护、未分类、运行中或共存（信道分类，5.2.5）。信息双向流动：CM 可按请求拉取数据，实体也可在自身信息变化时推送通告，使邻居保持最新。

blocks.en:
sequenceDiagram
  participant CE
  participant CM1 as CM
  participant CDIS
  CE->>CM1: SubscriptionRequest (5.2.1)
  CM1-->>CE: SubscriptionResponse
  CE->>CM1: CERegistrationRequest (5.2.2)
  CM1->>CDIS: CMRegistrationRequest
  CDIS-->>CM1: RegistrationResponse
  CM1->>CDIS: CoexistenceSetInformationRequest (5.2.3)
  CDIS-->>CM1: CoexistenceSetInformationResponse
blocks.zh:
sequenceDiagram
  participant CE
  participant CM1 as CM
  participant CDIS
  CE->>CM1: 订阅请求 (5.2.1)
  CM1-->>CE: 订阅响应
  CE->>CM1: CE注册请求 (5.2.2)
  CM1->>CDIS: CM注册请求
  CDIS-->>CM1: 注册响应
  CM1->>CDIS: 共存集信息请求 (5.2.3)
  CDIS-->>CM1: 共存集信息响应

blocks.en: Decisions need fresh data, so a CM can fetch arbitrary information — from a WSO via its CE, or directly from another CM (5.2.6) — and can ask a WSO to take radio measurements (5.2.7), either once or on a repeating schedule, with each report acknowledged. When two CMs must share a channel, they negotiate (5.2.8): they trade NegotiationRequest messages until one side reports success, optionally finishing with an announcement of who won which time slots. For tighter coordination they can elect a master CM and configure master/slave roles for centralised decision-making (5.2.9).
blocks.zh: 决策需要新鲜的数据，因此 CM 可以获取任意信息——通过 CE 向 WSO 获取，或直接向另一个 CM 获取 (5.2.6)——并可请求 WSO 进行无线电测量 (5.2.7)，一次性或按重复计划进行，每份报告都会被确认。当两个 CM 必须共享某信道时，它们进行协商 (5.2.8)：双方交换协商请求消息，直到一方报告成功，必要时以一条通告（说明谁赢得了哪些时隙）收尾。为实现更紧密的协调，它们还可推选一个主 CM 并配置主/从角色，用于集中式决策 (5.2.9)。

blocks.en: A coexistence decision only matters if it is carried out. Reconfiguration (5.2.10) lets a CM push a new channel or power setting to the WSOs it manages, or send the request to another CM that owns the affected WSO. Things also happen unexpectedly: an event procedure (5.2.11) lets a CE warn its CM about problems like degraded quality of service or a crossed signal-to-interference-plus-noise-ratio (SINR) threshold, and lets one CM forward such alerts to another. When a decision touches a network managed by a different CM, the coexistence set element reconfiguration procedure (5.2.12) carries the request across that boundary.
blocks.zh: 共存决策只有被执行才有意义。重配置 (5.2.10) 让 CM 把新的信道或功率设置下发给它所管理的 WSO，或把请求转交给拥有受影响 WSO 的另一个 CM。事情也会意外发生：事件流程 (5.2.11) 让 CE 就服务质量下降或越过信干噪比（SINR）门限等问题向其 CM 报警，并让一个 CM 将此类告警转发给另一个 CM。当某决策涉及由不同 CM 管理的网络时，共存集元素重配置流程 (5.2.12) 把请求跨越这一边界传递过去。

blocks.en: Notice the recurring shape: a Request/Response pair when an entity asks for something and waits, versus an Announcement/Confirm pair when an entity has new information to push out and just wants acknowledgement. Once you spot that pattern, every one of the twenty procedures reads the same way.
blocks.zh: 请注意反复出现的形态：当某实体主动询问并等待时，使用请求/响应这一对；当某实体有新信息要推送出去、只需对方确认时，使用通告/确认这一对。一旦看清这个模式，这二十个流程读起来就都是同一种套路。

blocks.en: Finally, networks come and go and systems span boundaries. The stop operation procedures (5.2.13) let a CE, a CM, or even a CDIS announce an orderly shutdown so peers can deregister and, if a CE loses its last CM, fall back gracefully. Inter-CM information exchange (5.2.14) shares coexistence-set data between CMs that follow different profiles, and the association procedures connect entities that need to talk: a CE associating with a CM (5.2.15), one CM associating with another (5.2.16), and that same association tunnelled across systems through the COE reference point (5.2.17). A CM can also learn what frequencies another network is on, directly (5.2.18) or again across systems over COE (5.2.19). And when a moving network's normal CM cannot serve it, the proxy coexistence service procedure (5.2.20) lets its previous CM relay a coexistence report on its behalf.
blocks.zh: 最后，网络有来有去，系统也会跨越边界。停止运行流程 (5.2.13) 让 CE、CM 乃至 CDIS 通告一次有序关闭，使对端得以注销；若某 CE 失去了最后一个 CM，也能平稳回退。CM 间信息交换 (5.2.14) 在遵循不同剖面的 CM 之间共享共存集数据；关联流程则把需要通信的实体连接起来：CE 与 CM 关联 (5.2.15)、一个 CM 与另一个 CM 关联 (5.2.16)，以及把同样的关联通过 COE 参考点跨系统隧道传输 (5.2.17)。CM 还能获知另一网络所在的频率，可直接获取 (5.2.18)，也可再次通过 COE 跨系统获取 (5.2.19)。而当某个移动中的网络无法被其正常的 CM 服务时，代理共存服务流程 (5.2.20) 让它先前的 CM 代为转交一份共存报告。

summary.en: Coverage map: all 21 units of clause 5.2 (5.1 + 5.2.1–5.2.20)

blocks.en: 5.1 General — scope of the procedures, the entities involved (CE, CM, COE, CDIS, plus external WSO/GCO and spectrum database), and the rule that exposed interfaces use TCP/IP and support both SSH and TLS. 5.2.1 Coexistence service subscription — WSO subscription, subscription update, CM-to-CDIS subscription, CM subscription update, and WSO subscription change. 5.2.2 Providing registration information — WSO/GCO registration, registration update, and WSO deregistration. 5.2.3 Obtaining coexistence-set information — obtaining/providing coexistence-set information from the CDIS, obtaining/providing coexistence-set element information between CMs, and obtaining/providing the coexistence report. 5.2.4 Obtaining available channel list from a WSO (via the CE). 5.2.5 Obtaining channel classification information — by CM from another CM, by CE from a CM, and announcing classification updates to a CM and to a CE. 5.2.6 Obtaining information — from a WSO and from another CM. 5.2.7 Requesting and obtaining measurements — request, one-time report, and scheduled reports. 5.2.8 Negotiation between CMs — without and with announcement. 5.2.9 Master CM selection — master/slave selection and master/slave configuration. 5.2.10 Reconfiguration — WSO reconfiguration and CM-to-CM reconfiguration request. 5.2.11 Event procedures — CE-to-CM and CM-to-CM event indications. 5.2.12 Coexistence set element reconfiguration (across CMs). 5.2.13 Stop operation — CE, CM, and CDIS stop procedures. 5.2.14 Inter-CM information exchange — providing and obtaining inter-CM information between different profiles. 5.2.15 CM association (CE to CM). 5.2.16 Inter-CM association (CM to CM across CDIS domains). 5.2.17 Inter-CM association over COE (across coexistence systems). 5.2.18 Obtaining operating frequency information (CM to CM). 5.2.19 Obtaining operating frequency information over COE (across systems). 5.2.20 Proxy coexistence service — a previous serving CM relays a coexistence report for a moving network.
blocks.zh: 5.1 总则——流程的范围、涉及的实体（CE、CM、COE、CDIS，以及外部的 WSO/GCO 和频谱数据库），以及暴露接口使用 TCP/IP 并同时支持 SSH 与 TLS 的规则。5.2.1 共存服务订阅——WSO 订阅、订阅更新、CM 向 CDIS 订阅、CM 订阅更新、WSO 订阅变更。5.2.2 提供注册信息——WSO/GCO 注册、注册更新、WSO 注销。5.2.3 获取共存集信息——从 CDIS 获取/提供共存集信息、在 CM 间获取/提供共存集元素信息、获取/提供共存报告。5.2.4 从 WSO 获取可用信道列表（经由 CE）。5.2.5 获取信道分类信息——CM 向另一 CM 获取、CE 向 CM 获取，以及向 CM 和向 CE 通告分类更新。5.2.6 获取信息——从 WSO 和从另一 CM。5.2.7 请求并获取测量——请求、一次性报告、定时报告。5.2.8 CM 间协商——不带通告与带通告。5.2.9 主 CM 选择——主/从选择与主/从配置。5.2.10 重配置——WSO 重配置与 CM 向 CM 的重配置请求。5.2.11 事件流程——CE 向 CM 与 CM 向 CM 的事件指示。5.2.12 共存集元素重配置（跨 CM）。5.2.13 停止运行——CE、CM、CDIS 的停止流程。5.2.14 CM 间信息交换——在不同剖面之间提供与获取 CM 间信息。5.2.15 CM 关联（CE 到 CM）。5.2.16 CM 间关联（跨 CDIS 域的 CM 到 CM）。5.2.17 经 COE 的 CM 间关联（跨共存系统）。5.2.18 获取运行频率信息（CM 到 CM）。5.2.19 经 COE 获取运行频率信息（跨系统）。5.2.20 代理共存服务——先前服务的 CM 为移动中的网络转交共存报告。

cites.note: Clause 5 Procedures and protocols: 5.1 General and the full set of 5.2 coexistence procedures (5.2.1 through 5.2.20).
